ddls:
CREATE TABLE "schools" (
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school */,
  "building" TEXT /* Name of the building where the school is located */,
  "enrollment" INTEGER /* Total number of students enrolled in the school */,
  "school_type" TEXT /* Type of the school (e.g., charter, traditional) */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* ID of the district the school belongs to */,
  "principal_name" TEXT /* Name of the principal */,
  "principal_email" TEXT /* Email of the principal */,
  "grade_levels" TEXT /* JSON array of grade levels offered */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the school was founded */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the school */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  "website" TEXT /* School's website URL */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the school's record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, staff, researcher) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "access_level" INTEGER /* Numerical value indicating the level of access */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school the data belongs to */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser used */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the user's session */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the access log record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)
